perturbation.original_text: As a material part of the consideration given and received by the parties in connection with the SPA: a. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: As a part of the consideration given and received by the parties in connection with the SPA: a. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'material' from 'material part of the consideration' reduces emphasis on the section's importance. This contradicts section 3, in the first line, stating 'As a material part of the consideration given and received by the parties:', thus creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a material part of the consideration given and received by the parties: a. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, expressly covenant and agree that for a period of one (1) year, Seller will not engage in any business or perform any service, directly or indirectly, in competition with the business of the Company, or have any interest, whether as proprietor, partner, employee, stockholder, principal, agent, consultant, director, officer, or in any other capacity or manner whatsoever, in any enterprise that shall engage in the business of the Company, except through publicly-traded shares of a corporation or mutual fund listed on a major stock exchange.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'As a material part of the consideration', while the changed text removes 'material', altering the emphasis and importance of the consideration. The contradicted text also uses the phrase 'As a material part of the consideration'. By removing the word 'material' in the changed text, it directly contradicts the contradicted text, as they both should be referring to the same thing but with different implications due to the removal of the word 'material', thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder expressly agree that in the event of any suit which may be brought by the Company for any violation of the provisions of this Agreement, any such breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall entitle the Company to any and/or of the following remedies: (aa) an order in any such suit enjoining Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder from violating said provisions. An order to that effect may be entered at any stage of such litigation, without the requirement to post bond, and any application for such injunction shall be without prejudice to any other right of action which may accrue to the parties by reason of the breach or threatened breach of this Agreement; and (bb) an order in any such suit providing for monetary damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder agree that in the event of any suit which may be brought by the Company for any violation of the provisions of this Agreement, any such breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall entitle the Company to any and/or of the following remedies: (aa) an order in any such suit enjoining Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder from violating said provisions. An order to that effect may be entered at any stage of such litigation, without the requirement to post bond, and any application for such injunction shall be without prejudice to any other right of action which may accrue to the parties by reason of the breach or threatened breach of this Agreement; and (bb) an order in any such suit providing for monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'expressly' from the sentence, we diminish the emphasis that the parties agree that the company is entitled to specific remedies in case of a breach. This creates a contradiction with the opening statement in 3.d.(i) where the parties acknowledge and 'expressly agree' to the terms, undermining the certainty of remedy enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (i) Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder understand that the provisions of this Agreement contain restrictive covenants and prohibit the disclosure of the Proprietary Property of the Company, agree to the reasonability of said provisions, and do herewith expressly agree and acknowledge that their breach of this Agreement will not be adequately compensated by money damages. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder acknowledge that the restrictions contained in this Agreement are a reasonable and necessary protection of the legitimate interests of the Company and that any violation of these restrictions would cause substantial irreparable injury to the Company. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder acknowledge that Purchaser would not have entered into this Agreement without receiving the consideration offered by Seller and Shareholder in binding itself to these restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'expressly' from the original text diminishes the emphasis on the parties' agreement regarding the company's entitlement to specific remedies, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that the parties 'expressly agree and acknowledge' that a breach of the agreement will not be adequately compensated by money damages. By removing 'expressly', the changed text weakens the agreement regarding remedies, thus contradicting the assertion in the contradicted text that the parties 'expressly agree'.

perturbation.original_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that in the course of ownership and/or employment with the Company that Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder have acquired and/or the Company has and will continue to provide Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder with, or access to information regarding the business, procedures, activities and services of the Company, including but not limited to, memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, customer accounts and customer lists, supplier lists, costs and prices of the Company, and customer needs, requirements and business affairs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”) as is necessary or desirable to assist him in his activities on behalf of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, acknowledge and agree that the Company has provided access to information regarding the business of the Company, including but not limited to customer lists, supplier lists, and customer needs (hereinafter referred to collectively as the “Proprietary Property”).
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the scope of what is considered 'Proprietary Property.' By removing the comprehensive list and reducing the information types, it contradicts the broader definition implied in section 3.d.(i), where 'restrictive covenants and prohibit the disclosure of the Proprietary Property' suggests a much wider range of protected information than now defined. Specifically, the omitted items listed create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (i) Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder understand that the provisions of this Agreement contain restrictive covenants and prohibit the disclosure of the Proprietary Property of the Company, agree to the reasonability of said provisions, and do herewith expressly agree and acknowledge that their breach of this Agreement will not be adequately compensated by money damages. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder acknowledge that the restrictions contained in this Agreement are a reasonable and necessary protection of the legitimate interests of the Company and that any violation of these restrictions would cause substantial irreparable injury to the Company. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder acknowledge that Purchaser would not have entered into this Agreement without receiving the consideration offered by Seller and Shareholder in binding itself to these restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Proprietary Property" by omitting key items (memorandum, files, forms, techniques, methods and procedures, programs, costs and prices of the Company) that were originally included. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which references 'Proprietary Property' in the context of restrictive covenants and prohibiting disclosure. The original text defined this term much more broadly, meaning the restrictive covenants would apply to a wider range of items than are now covered in the changed text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the scope of the restrictive covenants is now ambiguous and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: In furtherance of the foregoing and not in limitation thereof, Key Personnel and Shareholder agree that for a period of one (1) year, Key Personnel and Shareholder shall not (aa) directly or indirectly, solicit or service in any way, on behalf of itself or on behalf of or in conjunction with others, any customers, or prospective customers who have been solicited or serviced by the Company; (bb) directly or indirectly take any action which may induce any customer or divert any business from the Company; or (cc) directly or indirectly, for himself or any enterprise engaged in competition with the Company, solicit for employment or employ any employee who is then employed by the Company or who has been employed by the Company within one (1) year prior to the termination of his employment.
perturbation.changed_text: In furtherance of the foregoing and not in limitation thereof, Key Personnel and Shareholder agree that for a period of one (1) year, Key Personnel and Shareholder shall not (aa) directly or indirectly, solicit or service in any way, on behalf of itself or on behalf of or in conjunction with others, any customers, or prospective customers who have been solicited or serviced by the Company; (bb) directly or indirectly take any action which may induce any customer or divert any business from the Company; or (cc) solicit for employment or employ any employee who is then employed by the Company or who has been employed by the Company within one (1) year prior to the termination of his employment.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the phrase 'for himself or any enterprise engaged in competition with the Company' from (cc) weakens the restriction on soliciting employees. This change creates an internal contradiction because it could be interpreted that Key Personnel can solicit for companies who are not the Company directly. This creates confusion and runs counter to the overall intent of the non-compete agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a material part of the consideration given and received by the parties: a. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, expressly covenant and agree that for a period of one (1) year, Seller will not engage in any business or perform any service, directly or indirectly, in competition with the business of the Company, or have any interest, whether as proprietor, partner, employee, stockholder, principal, agent, consultant, director, officer, or in any other capacity or manner whatsoever, in any enterprise that shall engage in the business of the Company, except through publicly-traded shares of a corporation or mutual fund listed on a major stock exchange.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'for himself or any enterprise engaged in competition with the Company' from the non-solicitation clause (cc), weakening the restriction. The contradicted text states that Key Personnel shall not engage in any enterprise that shall engage in the business of the Company. The changed text allows for Key Personnel to solicit for companies not directly competitive with the Company, which contradicts the broader non-compete agreement outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language has been removed, creating a contradiction with the broader non-compete constraints.
